final_status: Pillars 1-5 substantially closed; NB17 cross-cutting synthesis complete

Pillar 1 \u2014 Patient stratification.key_finding: Four reproducible IBD ecotypes (E0/E1/E2/E3) on 8.5K cMD samples; HMP2 external-replication χ² p=0.016; UC Davis distributes E0 27% / E1 42% / E3 31% / E2 0% (χ² p=0.019)

Pillar 2 \u2014 Pathobiont identificatio.key_finding: Within-ecotype × within-substudy meta-analysis yields 51 E1 + 40 E3 Tier-A candidates; NB05 6 actionable Tier-A core; NB06 H2d 5/6 actionable in single per-subnet pathobiont module; HMP2 88.2% E1 sign-concordance external replication
Pillar 2 \u2014 Pathobiont identificatio.notebooks: NB04, NB04b, NB04c, NB04d, NB04e, NB04f, NB04g, NB04h, NB05, NB06

Pillar 3 \u2014 Functional drivers.key_finding: 8 H3 sub-hypotheses tested (5 SUPPORTED + 1 PARTIALLY + 2 PARTIAL + 1 NOT); 2 cross-corroborated 6-line mechanism narratives (iron + bile-acid); NB07d CC1 r=0.96 collapses all narratives into single joint axis (cliff δ=+0.50, p=4e-4)
Pillar 3 \u2014 Functional drivers.notebooks: NB07a, NB07b, NB07_v18, NB07c, NB07d, NB08a, NB09a, NB09b, NB09c, NB09d, NB10a, NB11

Pillar 4 \u2014 Phage targetability.key_finding: 3-layer phage-evidence convergence (literature + experimental + in-vivo); 5-phage E. coli AIEC cocktail at 94.7% strain coverage (PhageFoundry 17,672 pairs); H. hathewayi + F. plautii GAP confirmed across all 3 layers

Pillar 5 \u2014 UC Davis per-patient coc.key_finding: 14 of 23 patients (61%) with concrete cocktail drafts; pure phage cocktail structurally infeasible for E1 (3-strategy hybrid required); F. plautii BA-cost dominant E1 design constraint; patient 6967 E1→E3 drift validates state-dependent dosing rule (M. gnavus 14× expansion); patient 1112 reseq ρ=1.000 validates Kaiju reliability; 5 dosing rules + clinical-translation workflow

OvR-AUC vs per-patient agreement gap as classifier-utility diagnostic
Kaiju ↔ MetaPhlAn3 projection asymmetry — LDA robust, GMM fragile
Project-wide synonymy layer as reusable multi-cohort artifact
4-ecotype IBD framework with disease-stratifying signal on 8.5K samples
cMD substudy × diagnosis nesting as structural unidentifiability finding
Feature leakage in cluster-stratified DA as general methodological hazard
Within-ecotype × within-substudy meta-analysis as confound-free stratified design
Adversarial review as required complement to /berdl-review on nuanced projects
LOSO ARI as more honest stability metric than bootstrap ARI
Operationally-validated-Tier-A despite framework-variance pattern
Category schema choice as load-bearing methodological variable (regex → ontology)
Module-level metabolic-coupling-cost as Tier-A scoring extension
5-line cross-corroborated iron-acquisition narrative
Pathway-level vs metabolite-level signal can diverge in direction (pool ≠ flux)

Two independent 6-line cross-corroboration narratives in same project
Species-abundance-mediated vs strain-content-mediated CD-association as distinguishable mechanism axis

NB09b cross-cohort metabolomics replication establishes 9 strict + 3 theme-level replications
Multi-pillar mechanism narratives collapse to a single multi-omics joint factor (CC1 r=0.96)
3-layer phage-evidence convergence as Pillar-4 rigor pattern
Hybrid cocktail necessity in IBD ecotypes — pure phage cocktail not feasible for E1

Immediate (current cohort): Per-patient cocktail drafts for 23 UC Davis patients (NB15)
F. plautii BA-coupling-cost annotation per actionable target
Near-term (6\u201312 months): INPHARED + IMG/VR external DB queries for H. hathewayi / F. plautii / M. gnavus phages
AIEC strain-resolution diagnostic for the 8/23 E. coli-positive patients
M. gnavus qPCR validation as cheap ecotype-state proxy
Mid-term (12\u201324 months): Per-patient bile-acid panel for F. plautii BA-cost monitoring
Multi-cohort serology meta-analysis (firms up H3e PARTIAL)
Long-term (24+ months): Clinical pilot of hybrid 3-strategy cocktails (per-ecotype + per-patient + state-dependent)
Lytic-locked phage engineering for M. gnavus
GAG-degrading enzyme inhibitor screening for H. hathewayi

thesis_one_sentence: Crohn's disease at the gut-microbiome level is a single principal-direction phenomenon (NB07d CC1 r=0.96) within which 6 actionable Tier-A pathobionts and 2 cross-corroborated mechanism narratives (iron-acquisition + bile-acid 7α-dehydroxylation) define a state-dependent, hybrid-cocktail design framework with concrete per-patient drafts for 14 of 23 UC Davis CD patients.
remaining_out_of_scope: INPHARED + IMG/VR external DB queries for the 3 gut-anaerobe phage-coverage gaps
Multi-cohort prospective validation of state-dependent dosing rule
